trans-unit.source: Exercise 1: Part 1: Calculate vulnerability index
trans-unit.target: Exercise 1: Part 1: Calculate vulnerability index

trans-unit.source: Characteristics of the exercise
trans-unit.target: Characteristics of the exercise

trans-unit.source: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card}
trans-unit.target: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card}

trans-unit.source: Aim of the exercise
trans-unit.target: Aim of the exercise

trans-unit.source: We want to create an overview of different vulnerability indicators. Using a Covid-19 risk indicators dataset, we take `% permanent wall type`, `% permanent roof type` and `poverty incidence`. Using Uganda population statistics, we calculate the `% of under fives` and `% of elderly`. By combining the data, we are now able to visualise the areas in Uganda that are most vulnerable.
trans-unit.target: We want to create an overview of different vulnerability indicators. Using a Covid-19 risk indicators dataset, we take `% permanent wall type`, `% permanent roof type` and `poverty incidence`. Using Uganda population statistics, we calculate the `% of under fives` and `% of elderly`. By combining the data, we are now able to visualise the areas in Uganda that are most vulnerable.

trans-unit.source: Type of trainings exercise:
trans-unit.target: Type of trainings exercise:

trans-unit.source: This exercise can be used in online and presence training.
trans-unit.target: This exercise can be used in online and presence training.

trans-unit.source: It can be done as a follow-along exercise or individually as a self-study.
trans-unit.target: It can be done as a follow-along exercise or individually as a self-study.

trans-unit.source: Focus group (GIS-Knowledge Level)
trans-unit.target: Focus group (GIS-Knowledge Level)

trans-unit.source: These skills are relevant for
trans-unit.target: These skills are relevant for

trans-unit.source: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card}
trans-unit.target: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card}

trans-unit.source: Estimated time demand for the exercise.
trans-unit.target: Estimated time demand for the exercise.

trans-unit.source: Relevant Wiki Articles
trans-unit.target: Relevant Wiki Articles

trans-unit.source: [Geodata Import in QGIS](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.md)
trans-unit.target: [Geodata Import in QGIS](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.md)

trans-unit.target: [Projections](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projections_wiki.md)

trans-unit.target: [Geoprocessing](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_geoprocessing_wiki.md)

trans-unit.source: [Spatial Joins](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_spatial_joins_wiki.md)
trans-unit.target: [Spatial Joins](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_spatial_joins_wiki.md)

trans-unit.source: Instructions for the trainers
trans-unit.target: Instructions for the trainers

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} __Trainers Corner__
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} __Trainers Corner__

trans-unit.source: Prepare the training
trans-unit.target: Prepare the training

trans-unit.source: Take the time to familiarise yourself with the exercise and the provided material.
trans-unit.target: Take the time to familiarise yourself with the exercise and the provided material.

trans-unit.source: Prepare a white-board. It can be either a physical whiteboard, a flip-chart, or a digital whiteboard (e.g. Miro board) where the participants can add their findings and questions.
trans-unit.target: Prepare a white-board. It can be either a physical whiteboard, a flip-chart, or a digital whiteboard (e.g. Miro board) where the participants can add their findings and questions.

trans-unit.source: Before starting the exercise, make sure everybody has installed QGIS and has downloaded __and unzipped__ the data folder.
trans-unit.target: Before starting the exercise, make sure everybody has installed QGIS and has downloaded __and unzipped__ the data folder.

trans-unit.source: Check out [How to do trainings?](/content/Trainers_corner/en_how_to_training.md) for some general tips on training conduction
trans-unit.target: Check out [How to do trainings?](/content/Trainers_corner/en_how_to_training.md) for some general tips on training conduction

trans-unit.source: Conduct the training
trans-unit.target: Conduct the training

trans-unit.source: __Introduction:__
trans-unit.target: __Introduction:__

trans-unit.source: Introduce the idea and aim of the exercise.
trans-unit.target: Introduce the idea and aim of the exercise.

trans-unit.source: Provide the download link and make sure everybody has unzipped the folder before beginning the tasks.
trans-unit.target: Provide the download link and make sure everybody has unzipped the folder before beginning the tasks.

trans-unit.source: __Follow-along:__
trans-unit.target: __Follow-along:__

trans-unit.source: Show and explain each step yourself at least twice and slow enough so everybody can see what you are doing, and follow along in their own QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: Show and explain each step yourself at least twice and slow enough so everybody can see what you are doing, and follow along in their own QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: Make sure that everybody is following along and doing the steps themselves by periodically asking if anybody needs help or if everybody is still following.
trans-unit.target: Make sure that everybody is following along and doing the steps themselves by periodically asking if anybody needs help or if everybody is still following.

trans-unit.source: Be open and patient to every question or problem that might come up. Your participants are essentially multitasking by paying attention to your instructions and orienting themselves in their own QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: Be open and patient to every question or problem that might come up. Your participants are essentially multitasking by paying attention to your instructions and orienting themselves in their own QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: __Wrap up:__
trans-unit.target: __Wrap up:__

trans-unit.source: Leave time for any issues or questions concerning the tasks at the end of the exercise.
trans-unit.target: Leave time for any issues or questions concerning the tasks at the end of the exercise.

trans-unit.source: Leave some time for open questions.
trans-unit.target: Leave some time for open questions.

trans-unit.source: Data
trans-unit.target: Data

trans-unit.source: Download all datasets and save the folder on your computer and unzip the file. The zip folder includes:
trans-unit.target: Download all datasets and save the folder on your computer and unzip the file. The zip folder includes:

trans-unit.source: `uga_admbnda_adm2_ubos_20200824.shp`: [Uganda district boundaries (Admin level 2)](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/cod-ab-uga)
trans-unit.target: `uga_admbnda_adm2_ubos_20200824.shp`: [Uganda district boundaries (Admin level 2)](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/cod-ab-uga)

trans-unit.source: `COVID19_RISK_INDEX.shp`: [Covid-19 risk indicators](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/covid19_risk_index)
trans-unit.target: `COVID19_RISK_INDEX.shp`: [Covid-19 risk indicators](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/covid19_risk_index)

trans-unit.source: Task
trans-unit.target: Task

trans-unit.source: This first part of the exercise will prepare the data for subsequent non-spatial geodataprocessing, such as working with the attribute table. To calculate the vulnerability index, we will join all the relevant data using spatial geodataprocessing into a single vector layer.
trans-unit.target: This first part of the exercise will prepare the data for subsequent non-spatial geodataprocessing, such as working with the attribute table. To calculate the vulnerability index, we will join all the relevant data using spatial geodataprocessing into a single vector layer.

trans-unit.source: Load the Uganda district boundaries (admin level 2) (`uga_admbnda_adm2_ubos_20200824.shp`), as well as population statistics (`uga_admpop_adm2_2020proj_1y.csv`) and Covid-19 risk indicators (`COVID19_RISK_INDEX.shp`) into QGIS.
trans-unit.target: Load the Uganda district boundaries (admin level 2) (`uga_admbnda_adm2_ubos_20200824.shp`), as well as population statistics (`uga_admpop_adm2_2020proj_1y.csv`) and Covid-19 risk indicators (`COVID19_RISK_INDEX.shp`) into QGIS.

trans-unit.source: Make sure to reproject the dataset using the __district boundaries__ and the __Covid-19 risk indicators__ dataset into UTM zone 36N. Use the tool `Reproject layer` for this process. See the Wiki entry on [projections](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projections_wiki.md) for further information.
trans-unit.target: Make sure to reproject the dataset using the __district boundaries__ and the __Covid-19 risk indicators__ dataset into UTM zone 36N. Use the tool `Reproject layer` for this process. See the Wiki entry on [projections](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projections_wiki.md) for further information.

trans-unit.source: We can see that the polygons are different in shape and amount! It is likely that the risk data is using an older version of the admin boundaries. This is an issue we need to resolve in order to work properly with the data.
trans-unit.target: We can see that the polygons are different in shape and amount! It is likely that the risk data is using an older version of the admin boundaries. This is an issue we need to resolve in order to work properly with the data.

trans-unit.source: Screenshot of different sizes of the attribute tables :::
trans-unit.target: Screenshot of different sizes of the attribute tables :::

trans-unit.source: We will use the following solution for this problem:
trans-unit.target: We will use the following solution for this problem:

trans-unit.source: We can take the __closest district centroid__ (from the dataset with the most to the dataset with the fewest records). This is the solution we will use for this exercise as the difference between the two datasets is not drastically.
trans-unit.target: We can take the __closest district centroid__ (from the dataset with the most to the dataset with the fewest records). This is the solution we will use for this exercise as the difference between the two datasets is not drastically.

trans-unit.source: Calculate the ![](/fig/mAlgorithmCentroids.png) `Centroids` for the dataset containing the most elements, which are the district boundaries. You can find the tool under `Vector` → `Geometry Tools` → `Centroids`. See the Wiki entry on [Geoprocessing](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_geoprocessing_wiki.md) for further information.
trans-unit.target: Calculate the ![](/fig/mAlgorithmCentroids.png) `Centroids` for the dataset containing the most elements, which are the district boundaries. You can find the tool under `Vector` → `Geometry Tools` → `Centroids`. See the Wiki entry on [Geoprocessing](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_geoprocessing_wiki.md) for further information.

trans-unit.source: Edit the points so they are inside the correct polygons. This is necessary because the __centroid of a polygon may fall outside of it__ when it has an __unusual shape__. To move a centroid that is outside its boundaries into the district boundaries, first activate the `Toggle editing mode` button, which can be found by clicking on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) while activating the centroid layer. Then, select the ![](/fig/mActionMoveFeaturePoint.png) `Move Feature` tool. Search for the centroid that is outside its boundaries and move it to the appropriate district boundary. Save the changes and end the editing mode.
trans-unit.target: Edit the points so they are inside the correct polygons. This is necessary because the __centroid of a polygon may fall outside of it__ when it has an __unusual shape__. To move a centroid that is outside its boundaries into the district boundaries, first activate the `Toggle editing mode` button, which can be found by clicking on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) while activating the centroid layer. Then, select the ![](/fig/mActionMoveFeaturePoint.png) `Move Feature` tool. Search for the centroid that is outside its boundaries and move it to the appropriate district boundary. Save the changes and end the editing mode.

trans-unit.source: The black points represent the centroids of the features of the input layer. The red circle indicates the centroid that requires editing. :::
trans-unit.target: The black points represent the centroids of the features of the input layer. The red circle indicates the centroid that requires editing. :::

trans-unit.source: There is an issue that is produced when joining the datasets, but it can be solved by using the `Fix geometries` tool on the Covid-19 risk dataset.
trans-unit.target: There is an issue that is produced when joining the datasets, but it can be solved by using the `Fix geometries` tool on the Covid-19 risk dataset.

trans-unit.source: Screenshot on how to fix the geometries. :::
trans-unit.target: Screenshot on how to fix the geometries. :::

trans-unit.source: Use the tool `Join attributes by location` to join the Covid-19 risk polygons onto the centroids. As a spatial relationship select `within` and select the columns `%permrooft`, `%permwallt` and `Povertyinc` as the fields that should be added. See the Wiki entry on [spatial joins](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_spatial_joins_wiki.md) for further information.
trans-unit.target: Use the tool `Join attributes by location` to join the Covid-19 risk polygons onto the centroids. As a spatial relationship select `within` and select the columns `%permrooft`, `%permwallt` and `Povertyinc` as the fields that should be added. See the Wiki entry on [spatial joins](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_spatial_joins_wiki.md) for further information.

trans-unit.source: Screenshot of Join attribute by location operation. :::
trans-unit.target: Screenshot of Join attribute by location operation. :::

trans-unit.source: Use the tool `Join attributes by location` again to join the previously enriched points onto the Uganda district boundaries. Now select "contain" as the spatial relationship and again select the same three columns for joining.
trans-unit.target: Use the tool `Join attributes by location` again to join the previously enriched points onto the Uganda district boundaries. Now select "contain" as the spatial relationship and again select the same three columns for joining.

trans-unit.source: Screenshot of the second Join attribute by location operation. :::
trans-unit.target: Screenshot of the second Join attribute by location operation. :::

trans-unit.source: The next steps of the vulnerability index calculation will be completed in the second part of this exercise, the Non-spatial Geodataprocessing section. Please refer to the [provided link](/content/Module_6/en_qgis_non_spatial_tools_ex2.md) for this exercise.
trans-unit.target: The next steps of the vulnerability index calculation will be completed in the second part of this exercise, the Non-spatial Geodataprocessing section. Please refer to the [provided link](/content/Module_6/en_qgis_non_spatial_tools_ex2.md) for this exercise.
